title: Deterministic Repair Contracts
description: Specifies the structure, constraints, and audit requirements for all recovery repair operations. Ensures every repair is auditable, replayable, deterministic, and lineage-preserving.
purpose: Eliminate untracked, ad hoc, or silent state repair by mandating formal repair contracts for every recovery mutation.

invariants.description: Every mutation during recovery must have a corresponding repair contract.
No repair may contain a forbidden operation.

repair_contract_structure.description: Every repair operation must be documented as a repair contract with these mandatory fields.

required_fields.description: Unique repair identifier within incident scope.
Reference to the incident that triggered this repair.
Reference to the drift classification that this repair addresses.
The authoritative source that determines the correct state (e.g., ticket JSON, journal, checkpoint).
State that must be true before repair executes.
State that must be true after repair executes.
Exact sequence of mutations applied. Each entry must specify file, path, old_value, new_value.
Whether this repair can be rolled back using the mutation_log.
Governance event appended to journal recording this repair.
Identifier of the agent or operator that executed the repair.

forbidden_operations.description: Operations that are strictly forbidden during recovery repair.

operations.description: Mutating a file without recording the exact old and new values in the mutation_log.
operations.rationale: Silent overwrites destroy auditability and prevent rollback.

operations.description: Applying unstructured JSON patches without a formal contract, schema validation, or mutation_log.
operations.rationale: Ad hoc patching is non-deterministic and non-replayable.

operations.description: Directly modifying execution-lock.json outside the formal lock acquisition/release protocol.
operations.rationale: Lock state must always be consistent with active execution. Direct mutation bypasses guards.

operations.description: Any repair action that does not generate a repair_id, mutation_log, and audit_event.
operations.rationale: Untracked actions are invisible to replay, audit, and forensic analysis.

operations.description: Performing a destructive restore operation without first creating a recoverable snapshot.
operations.rationale: Destructive operations must always be reversible.

operations.description: Modifying or deleting a checkpoint without explicit audit trail and incident_id.
operations.rationale: Checkpoints are immutable recovery anchors. Mutation corrupts replay lineage.

audit_lineage_requirements.description: Every repair must produce a complete audit lineage.

requirements.description: Snapshot of all affected files before repair execution.
requirements.method: JSON stringify and hash of pre-repair file contents

requirements.description: Ordered list of every mutation with file path, JSON pointer, old value, new value.
requirements.method: RFC 6902 JSON Patch or equivalent structured diff

requirements.description: Snapshot of all affected files after repair execution.
requirements.method: JSON stringify and hash of post-repair file contents

requirements.description: Append-only event to governance journal with repair_id, incident_id, drift_reference, timestamp.
Post-repair assertion that post_condition is satisfied.
requirements.method: automated validation script against post_condition schema

determinism_rules.description: Rules ensuring repair operations are deterministic.

rules.description: Repair must derive target state exclusively from the declared authority_source. No improvised values.
Multiple repairs within an incident must execute in repair_id order. No parallel repair execution.
Each repair must be idempotent. Re-executing the same repair on already-repaired state must be a no-op.
All mutated files must validate against their schemas after repair.
Repair execution must not depend on external state (network, time-of-day, randomness). Clock is allowed for timestamps only.
